title: Lush Norwegian Cucumber Broth
description: A silky, grassy broth made with Persian cucumber, herbs, and a cauldron cook, perfect for a macrobiotic-friendly one-pot meal.
instructions: Heat the cauldron over medium heat and add the water.
Add the diced carrot, celery, and thinly sliced onion to the water and bring to a gentle simmer.
Stir in the sea salt and let the vegetables cook for 10 minutes until they start to soften.
Add the sliced Persian cucumbers and let the broth simmer for another 8 minutes, allowing the cucumber to release a natural gelatinous texture.
Remove the cauldron from heat and stir in the chopped dill and fresh lemon juice.
Taste and adjust seasoning with additional salt or lemon juice if desired.
Let the broth rest for 5 minutes to develop a deeper grassy flavor.
Serve the broth hot in individual bowls, spooning a few cucumber slices into each.
Optionally garnish with a sprig of dill or a dash of sea salt.
Enjoy the light, glassy-jelly broth as a soothing, macrobiotic-friendly lunch.
